inputs.label: Project Duration (months)
inputs.businessContext: Standard project timeline; typical range 1-120 months based on PMI guidelines.

inputs.label: Team Size (FTE)
inputs.businessContext: Full-time equivalent staff; common in resource planning per PMBOK.

inputs.label: Average Hourly Rate ($)
inputs.businessContext: Weighted average labor cost; industry benchmarks from BLS or internal data.

inputs.label: Hours per Week per FTE
inputs.businessContext: Standard workweek; 40 hours typical, overtime possible up to 80.

inputs.label: Weeks per Month
inputs.businessContext: Average weeks per month (52/12 ≈ 4.33); used for monthly cost calculation.

inputs.label: Material Cost ($)
inputs.businessContext: Direct material expenses; includes raw materials, components, supplies.

inputs.label: Equipment Cost ($)
inputs.businessContext: Capital equipment or rental costs; depreciation may be considered.

inputs.label: Overhead Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Indirect cost allocation; typical range 10-50% based on industry (e.g., construction, IT).

inputs.label: Contingency Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Risk buffer; recommended 5-20% per PMI risk management.

inputs.label: Profit Margin (%)
inputs.businessContext: Target profit; varies by industry (e.g., 10-20% typical).

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Subjective confidence in input data; affects adjusted cost (low=+20%, medium=0%, high=-10%).

thresholds.overheadRate > 50: Overhead rate exceeds typical maximum; verify allocation.
thresholds.contingencyRate > 20: Contingency rate high; consider risk reassessment.
thresholds.profitMargin > 30: Profit margin unusually high; check market competitiveness.
thresholds.avgHourlyRate > 150: Hourly rate above typical; ensure justification.

suggestedActions: If overhead rate > 50%, review indirect cost allocation and reduce non-essential overhead.
If contingency rate > 20%, perform detailed risk analysis to justify or reduce buffer.
If profit margin > 30%, benchmark against industry standards to ensure competitiveness.
If hourly rate > 150, consider using blended rates or outsourcing lower-skill tasks.
